tracks.meaning: to create, shape, or mold something into a particular structure or configuration
the visible shape and structure of a person's body or face
a distinct variety or subspecies of an organism
a printed document with blank spaces to be filled in with information
the structure or layout of something such as an essay, argument, or composition
a data structure in software or a template used for organizing information
a temporary wooden or metal framework used as a support during building
a class or group organized by academic level or ability in a school
to arrange, organize, or put together in a structured way
an official document or legal instrument with standard terms and blanks to complete
the shape or physical appearance of a medical condition or symptom
the essential nature or underlying structure of something abstract or conceptual
the physical arrangement and layout of text and images on a printed page
a pattern of behavior or mental arrangement that develops through experience
an organized filing system or container designed to hold and organize items
the physical stage setting or backdrop representing a location in a performance
